title: Passiflora urceolata
intro: Passiflora urceolata, also known as the wine passionflower, is a species of flowering plant in the Passifloraceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anxiolytic and sedative, making it a potential treatment for anxiety and insomnia. Passiflora urceolata contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Passiflora urceolata is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its leaves and flowers are also sometimes dried and used as a calming herbal tea.
taxonomy: Passiflora urceolata is a species of passion flower native to South America. It belongs to the family Passifloraceae, commonly known as the passionflower family. Within this family, Passiflora urceolata is classified in the genus Passiflora, which comprises over 550 species of passion flowers. The specific classification for Passiflora urceolata is Passiflora urceolata, with no subspecies recognized. This classification is based on the plant's unique characteristics and morphology.
